statement: Older users are inherently incapable with technology, so interfaces for them should be dumbed down.
why_it_spreads: Ageist stereotyping and conflation of unfamiliarity or accessibility needs with incapacity.
evidence_against: NN/g research on older adults (src-nngroup): older users succeed when designs respect target size, contrast and clear language; failures stem from poor design, not age.
W3C WAI / WCAG (src-w3c-wai, src-wcag22): age-related needs are addressed by sound accessibility practice, not by removing capability.
replacement_principle: Design for the actual physical, sensory and cognitive needs present in the audience (vision, motor precision, familiarity) instead of stereotyping a demographic.
